codeReview.summary: The feature adds the requested CI typecheck, lint, and optional read-only smoke steps, wires them to the dualvm working directory, and sources smoke-test addresses from the canonical manifest. I found no blocking correctness problems, but there are two non-blocking robustness gaps in the new lint and smoke-script implementation.

issues.description: The new lint config ignores `**/*.mjs` and `**/*.js`, so `npm run lint` does not check `scripts/ci-smoke.mjs` or any other Node helper script. That weakens the CI lint step introduced by this feature because the newly added smoke script is outside lint coverage.

The smoke script always prefers `manifest.polkadotHubTestnet.rpcUrl` and never falls back to `process.env.POLKADOT_HUB_TESTNET_RPC_URL` or `manifest.polkadotHubTestnet.fallbackRpcUrl`. When the primary public RPC is flaky, operators cannot redirect the smoke check to an override/fallback endpoint without editing the manifest, which is inconsistent with existing script/env conventions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The docs-worker procedure says CI changes should be locally verified with `npm test && npx tsc --noEmit && npm run build`, but this feature's recorded verification omitted `npm run build` while the handoff still claimed 'All local verification passes' and `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` was set to true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/docs-worker/SKILL.md:44-47 requires a local build for CI changes; handoff 2026-03-17T12-40-58-222Z__ci-enhancement__e3d6307f-21c3-4b0c-812a-f281a4acee01.json:10-10 claims all local verification passed, but lines 14-34 list only npm test, tsc, lint, and ci-smoke.

summary: Reviewed commit a68bd13b57133776e08be4a67694e20b118b713e for ci-enhancement. The implementation satisfies the requested CI additions and manifest-driven smoke check with no blocking defects found, so the feature passes review, but lint coverage for `.mjs` scripts and smoke-script RPC override/fallback behavior should be tightened in a follow-up.
